api_instructions.before_you_begin.conten: Ensure you have a valid application key obtained on the API URL integration page.
Ensure you have your unique signing key. This is used to sign your requests to the MarketRaker API.
This key should be kept secret and should not be shared with anyone as it is used to verify your identity.
MarketRaker does not store your signing key and will not be able to retrieve it for you. If you lose your signing key, you will need to generate a new one.
api_instructions.description.content_1: The MarketRaker API allows you to retrieve historical trading indicators for a given trading pair and timeframe. The endpoint searches for the most recent indicator in the 12 hours prior to the specified timestamp.
api_instructions.description.content_2: Each request to the MarketRaker API will cost one credit. To learn more about credits, please see our Pricing page.
api_instructions.example_code.content: We have provided a sample Python script that demonstrates how to make a request to the MarketRaker API.
api_instructions.follow_the_steps: Follow the steps to complete the integration setup
api_instructions.making_requests.content: To make a request to the MarketRaker API, you will need to sign your request using your signing key and include the appropriate headers.
See the full example code in the next section followed by the response codes.
Construct the request URL with your desired trading pair and the Unix timestamp in seconds. The timestamp refers to the end of the 12 hour timeframe in which we will search for the most recent indicator. Use the following format for the request URL:
Generate a signature using your signing key and the data string composed of your `trading_pair`, `request_timestamp_seconds`, the current Unix timestamp in seconds, and your application ID.
Include the following headers in your request:
Send the GET request to the MarketRaker API URL with the appropriate headers using a library like 'requests' in Python.
api_instructions.notification_format.con: When a new trading indicator is sent as a notification, we will use your registered webhook URL to send the indicator to you. You will receive an HTTP request similar to the one below.
The request body will contain the indicator with the relevant attributes which you can extract and use.
api_instructions.pricing.content_1: Each request made to the MarketRaker API will cost one credit.
api_instructions.pricing.content_2: However, for the time being, we are offering free API access to all users. This means that you will not be charged any credits for making requests to the MarketRaker API. Note that the free API access is subject to change in the future.
api_instructions.response_format.content: The API will return a JSON response that includes a `success` flag, `msg`, `msgType`, and `records`. The response can be interpreted as follows:
If `success` is `True` and msgType is ‘Success’, there should be a record present from which you can retrieve the indicator data.
If ‘success’ is ‘True’ and msgType is ‘Notice’, the attached message will give more information, but most likely no indicators could be found for the 12 hours prior to the request timestamp.
If `success` is `False`, refer to the `msg` and `msgType` for details on the error or notice provided.

api_instructions.security.content_1: We have implemented an optional security feature that you can use to ensure the authenticity of the trading indicators received by your webhook. We have signed the request body using RSA. You can retrieve this signature by accessing the 'X-Signature' header of the HTTP request.
api_instructions.security.content_2: Verifying the signature will provide the following assurances:
api_instructions.security.content_2.list: 1. The trading indicator was indeed sent by MarketRaker.
2. The trading indicator was not modified or altered in any way after we sent the HTTP request to you.
api_instructions.security.content_3: To verify the signature you will first need to retrieve the verification key provided on the API URL integration page. You can use this verification key to verify our signature.
api_instructions.security.content_4: Provided below is basic sample code that uses the cryptography module in Python to verify the signature.
api_instructions.step_1.content_1: The API URL integration allows you to register a webhook or interface with MarketRaker's own API.
api_instructions.step_1.content_2: When registering a webhook, trading indicators will be sent to this endpoint as a JSON object. See Notification Format for more details regarding this.
api_instructions.step_1.content_3: We also provide a way for you to verify the authenticity of the indicator via a signature. Please see Security for details.
api_instructions.step_1.content_4: When interfacing with the MarketRaker API, you can send signed requests to our API to retrieve historical trading indicators.
api_instructions.step_1.content_5: Each request send to our API will cost one credit. To learn more about credits, please see our Pricing page.
